events.text: Patient is a 41-year-old Hispanic female with a BMI of 24.1. No family history of breast cancer. BRCA1/2 negative.

At age 40, patient discovered a right breast mass during self-examination. Biopsy confirmed invasive ductal carcinoma (8500/3) of the right breast NOS (C50.9), grade 3, ER negative, PR negative, HER2 IHC 3+, Ki-67 70%.
At age 40, right breast core needle biopsy: invasive ductal carcinoma, Nottingham grade 3, triple-negative phenotype except HER2 positive. ER negative (
At age 40, bilateral diagnostic mammogram showed a 4.8 cm irregular mass in the right breast with associated pleomorphic calcifications, BIRADS 5. Left breast unremarkable.
At age 40, MRI breast confirmed a 5.1 cm enhancing mass with ipsilateral axillary lymphadenopathy. Multiple suspicious axillary nodes right.
At age 40, PET/CT staging showed hypermetabolic right breast mass (SUVmax 12.4), right axillary lymph nodes (SUVmax 8.2), and a solitary 1.5 cm left hepatic lobe lesion (SUVmax 6.1).
At age 40, ultrasound-guided liver biopsy confirmed metastatic breast carcinoma, HER2 IHC 3+. Consistent with primary breast tumor.
At age 40, de novo stage IV (cT3N2M1) HER2-positive, ER-negative, PR-negative breast cancer with liver metastasis.
At age 40, tumor genomic profiling (FoundationOne CDx) revealed TP53 splice site mutation, ERBB2 amplification (copy number 18), and CDK12 loss. TMB 6 mutations/Mb. MSS.
At age 40, started first-line TCHP (docetaxel 75 mg/m2, carboplatin AUC 6, trastuzumab, pertuzumab) every 21 days.
At age 40, baseline echocardiogram LVEF 65%.
At age 41, after cycle 2 of TCHP, developed grade 3 neutropenia requiring dose delay of 1 week. Received pegfilgrastim prophylaxis for subsequent cycles.
At age 41, grade 2 nausea and grade 2 diarrhea on TCHP. Managed with ondansetron and loperamide.
At age 41, restaging CT after 4 cycles of TCHP showed complete resolution of the liver lesion and decrease in primary breast mass to 2.1 cm. Axillary nodes decreased in size.

At age 41, excellent response to TCHP. Discussed surgical options for the breast given oligometastatic presentation with complete response in liver. Multidisciplinary tumor board recommended continuing systemic therapy.
At age 41, completed 6 cycles TCHP then transitioned to maintenance trastuzumab and pertuzumab every 21 days.
At age 41, PET/CT showed complete metabolic response. No FDG-avid disease identified. Clinical complete response achieved.
At age 41, LVEF 60% on surveillance echocardiogram. Mild asymptomatic decline. Continued therapy.
At age 41, patient doing well on maintenance HP. ECOG 0. No significant toxicities. Counseled regarding fertility preservation options given young age and good prognosis.
At age 41, MRI brain screening negative for intracranial metastases.
At age 41, grade 1 fatigue and intermittent grade 1 diarrhea on pertuzumab maintenance. Tolerable.

scenario_blurb: Metastatic HER2-positive breast cancer. IHC 3+ or FISH amplified. Treated with trastuzumab-based regimens (THP, T-DM1, trastuzumab deruxtecan). Include hormone receptor status variation (ER+/HER2+ and ER-/HER2+). Some with brain metastases.
